sentence_reviews.text: An observe–plan–act loop maintains state, selects tools or actions, processes feedback, and iterates until a goal, budget, or safety condition is reached.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: ReAct arXiv abstract, lines 42-46: interleaved reasoning traces and task-specific actions, plan updating, external sources/environments, Wikipedia API and interactive decision-making tasks. AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts. Sutton & Barto, Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction, agent-environment interaction framing and feedback/reward-loop concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: State/action/feedback iteration is supported across agent/RL/ReAct sources; explicit budgets and safety stop conditions are atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, Tool use & function calling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Observe–plan–act agent loops stores, selects, or obtains information through Context windows & working memory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: ReAct arXiv abstract, lines 42-46: interleaved reasoning traces and task-specific actions, plan updating, external sources/environments, Wikipedia API and interactive decision-making tasks. AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts. Sutton & Barto, Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction, agent-environment interaction framing and feedback/reward-loop concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Maintaining state/history is required by iterative agent loops, though not directly phrased as context windows.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Observe–plan–act agent loops is modeled as depending on Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, Tool use & function calling and feeding downstream nodes such as Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops, Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets, Skill libraries, workflows & procedural plans.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
